verdict.answer_first: A states the immediate action early: "Turn on automated backups today" and "Take a manual snapshot right now"; B starts with "Step 0 — Confirm reality first" before the fix.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a concrete emergency path: "Aurora PostgreSQL (production) — do this first"; B surveys multiple stores and asks "Which database did you actually mean".
verdict.fabrication: Both invent context; A says "production Aurora PostgreSQL" and "local on this Mac (sqlite `index.db` / neo4j)", while B adds "revenue/users/UTM", "PostHog", and "caffeinate-wrapped".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on immediately because it gives the shortest ordered sequence: enable automated backups, take manual snapshot, set RPO/RTO, add pg_dump, test restore.
